body:
## Summary
- Add a committed `.agent/sepo-version.json` install metadata file.
- Document Sepo SemVer policy and installed source-identity fields.
- Update setup/install docs to tell users to keep the metadata file with `.agent/`.

This is a minimal alternative to #128 for #127 / #74. It intentionally does not add a reader, CLI, validation library, tests, updater rewriting, bug-report integration, or release workflows yet. Those can be added in the later PRs that actually consume the metadata.

## Tests
- `python3 -m json.tool .agent/sepo-version.json >/dev/null`
- `git diff --check`

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `review` (round 2/10).

manual orchestrate start on PR; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777870125116 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUzMDE1ODA2MDk6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6MTMwOnJldmlldzoxMzA6Mg -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | The PR adds discrete metadata fields in `.agent/sepo-version.json`: `schema_version`, `version`, `channel`, `source_repo`, `source_ref`, `source_sha`, `installed_from`, and `agent_files_hash`, instead of encoding source identity into one overloaded string. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | The diff updates `.agent/docs/deployment/install-existing-repository.md`, `.agent/docs/deployment/setup-guide.md`, and `.agent/docs/README.md`, and adds `.agent/docs/technical-details/versioning.md` to document the new metadata file and SemVer/source-identity policy. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The PR body explicitly ties this to #127 / #74 and scopes it as a minimal alternative to #128. The diff matches the parent #74 policy direction: SemVer, pre-release `v0.x`, source identity fields, and install docs. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | The new docs include a metadata field table, not a per-file inventory table. They describe policy and schema semantics rather than listing repository files and purposes. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to one metadata JSON file and directly related documentation. It intentionally avoids reader/CLI/validation/updater/bug-report/release workflow changes. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | This is the smallest foundation slice: committed metadata plus docs. It avoids new runtime code, dependencies, validation layers, tests, and workflow automation that the PR body explicitly defers. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric fit is scope control: this PR is narrower than #128 and matches the requested minimal foundation.
- I did not score unrelated authorization, orchestration, idempotency, preview, status-comment, merge-flow, or runtime failure rubrics because this PR does not change those behaviors.
- I verified the live PR metadata and diff with `gh pr view` / `gh pr diff`. A local `python3 -m json.tool .agent/sepo-version.json` could not be run because the local checkout was not on the PR head and did not contain the new file; the PR diff itself shows valid JSON structure.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #130 adds a committed `.agent/sepo-version.json` metadata file and documents Sepo SemVer/source-identity policy.
- It is a narrower alternative to #128 for the version-management plan in #74 / #127.
- The PR intentionally limits this slice to metadata plus docs, leaving readers, validation helpers, CLI output, tests, updater behavior, bug-report wiring, and release workflows for later work.
- The change gives future install/update/reporting work a shared metadata contract without adding runtime behavior yet.

## Review
The synthesized review finds no required branch-change defects. Claude raised caution around deferred validation/tests and the still-open overlap with #128, while Codex and the rubrics review treated both as intentional scope or maintainer sequencing rather than code issues.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable branch issues | INFO | The metadata schema and docs are coherent, scoped, and aligned with #74. Remaining concerns are optional follow-up or human sequencing decisions, not required fixes to this branch. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific findings.
- The PR body already acknowledges the intentionally deferred work: reader, CLI, validation library, tests, updater rewriting, bug-report integration, and release workflows.
- Existing inline review comments are empty; no new inline comment was posted because the synthesized concerns are not concrete changed-line defects.
- Reviewer disagreement was resolved in favor of the PR’s explicit minimal scope.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: no unresolved required branch-change work remains for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #130.

no rubric changes

I found no trusted human review/comment feedback on PR #130 that warranted a new durable rubric. The PR conversation is agent-authored status/review output only, and the broader linked context mainly reinforces preferences already covered by active rubrics: small scoped changes, reading linked context, docs in sync, simple implementation, and structured metadata. No files were changed under `agent-rubrics`.

title: Add simplified Sepo version metadata foundation
